title: Converts Temperature between Celsius and Fahrenheit
code:
n = int(input())

if(n == 1):
	c = float(input())
	f = (c*9/5) + 32
	print(f"{f:.2f}""°F")
	
elif(n == 2):
	f = float(input())
	c = (f-32)*(5/9)
	print(f"{c:.2f}""°C")

else:
	print("Invalid choice")

title: Functions to Calculate Area and Perimeter
Random Password Generator
code:
import random

def generate_random_password(seed_value):

	random.seed(seed_value)
	passlen = random.randint(7,10)
	pas = ""
	for i in range(passlen):
		asc = random.randint(33,126)
		pas += chr(asc)
	return pas

seed_value = int(input())
random_password = generate_random_password(seed_value)
print("Randomly generated password: {}".format(random_password))

title: Palindrome or Not
code:
#By for loop:
# n = input()
# r = ""
# for i in range(len(n)-1,-1,-1):
# 	r += n[i]
# if n == r:
# 	print("Palindrome")
# else:
# 	print("Not a Palindrome")

#By two pointers:

str = input()
l = 0
r = len(str) - 1
flag = True

while (l <= r):
	if (str[l] != str[r]):
		flag = False
		break
	l = l+1
	r = r-1

if(flag):
	print("Palindrome")
else:
	print("Not a Palindrome")

title: Sort the List
code:
# write your code...
# l = list(map(int,input().split(",")))
# a = sorted(l)
# b = sorted(l,reverse = True)
# print(a)
# print(b)

#Using bubble sort

l = list(map(int,input().split(",")))
temp = 0
for i in range(len(l)):
	for j in range(len(l)-1):
		if(l[j] > l[j+1]):
			temp = l[j]
			l[j] = l[j+1]
			l[j+1] = temp

print(l)
print(l[::-1])

title: Base Conversion
code:
# Type your code here.
def base(n,b,fb):
	num = int(n,b)
	s = ""
	if num == 0:
		return 0
	decimal = "0123456789ABCDEF"
	while(num != 0):
		r = num%fb
		s = decimal[r] + s
		num = num // fb
	return(s)

n = input().strip()
b = int(input())
fb = int(input())
print(base(n,b,fb))

title: Bank Account Modeling using Classes
code:
# Account class
class Account:
	def __init__(self,account_number,balance):
		self.account_number = account_number
		self.balance = balance

	def deposit(self,amount):
		self.balance += amount
		print(f"Balance after deposit: {self.balance}")

	def withdraw(self,amount):
		if(amount <= self.balance):
			self.balance -= amount
			print(f"Balance after withdrawal: {self.balance}")
		else:
			print("Insufficient funds!")

	def display_balance(self):
		print(f"Current balance: {self.balance}")


# Customer class
class Customer:
	def __init__(self,name,account):
		self.name = name
		self.account = account

	def display_customer(self):
		print("Customer Details:")
		print(f"Customer Name: {self.name}")
		print(f"Account Number: {self.account.account_number}")
		print(f"Current balance: {self.account.balance}")

# Bank class
class Bank:
	def __init__(self,name):
		self.name = name 
		self.customer = None

	def set_customer(self,customer):
		self.customer = customer

	def display_customer(self):
		print(f"Customer in {self.name} Bank {self.customer.name}")

bank_name = input("Bank name: ")
cus_name = input("Customer name: ")
acc_number = input("Account number: ")
ini_balance = float(input("Initial balance: "))
amount_deposit = float(input("Amount to Deposit: "))

a = Account(acc_number,ini_balance)
b = Customer(cus_name,a)
c = Bank(bank_name)
a.deposit(amount_deposit)
amount_withdraw = float(input("Amount to Withdraw: "))
a.withdraw(amount_withdraw)
b.display_customer()

title: School Management System using Classes
code:
# Student class
class Student:
	def __init__(self, name, roll_number):
		self.name = name
		self.roll_number = roll_number

	def display_student(self):
		print("Student Details:")
		print("Student Name:",self.name)
		print("Roll Number:",self.roll_number)


# Teacher class
class Teacher:
	def __init__(self, name, subject):
		self.name = name
		self.subject = subject

	def display_teacher(self):
		print("Teacher Details:")
		print("Teacher Name:",self.name)
		print("Subject:",self.subject)


# Course class
class Course:
	def __init__(self, course_name, teacher, student):
		self.course_name = course_name
		self.t = teacher
		self.s = student

	def display_course(self):
		print("Course Details:")
		print("Course Name:",self.course_name)
		print("Teacher:",self.t.name)
		print("Student:",self.s.name)


sname = input("Student name: ")
sroll = input("Student roll number: ")
tname = input("Teacher name: ")
sub = input("Subject: ")
cname = input("Course name: ")

st = Student(sname, sroll)
st.display_student()

tea = Teacher(tname,sub)
tea.display_teacher()

cou = Course(cname, tea, st)
cou.display_course()

title: Flask Web Application to Display MongoDB Collection Data in Tabular Format
code:
from flask import Flask, render_template
from pymongo import MongoClient

app = Flask(__name__)

# MongoDB Connection (already provided in your environment)
client = MongoClient("mongodb://mongo:27017/")   # Change only if needed
db = client["labdb"]                                 # Database name
collection = db["students"]                           # Collection name

# Complete the route functionality
@app.route('/viewdata')
def view_data():
	records = collection.find()
	data = list(records)
	return render_template("table.html", data=data)

if __name__ == "__main__":
	app.run(host="0.0.0.0", debug=True)

title: Dynamic Template Rendering
code:
from pathlib import Path

BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent

SECRET_KEY = 'demo-secret-key'
DEBUG = True
ALLOWED_HOSTS = ["*"]

INSTALLED_APPS = [
    'django.contrib.admin',
    'django.contrib.auth',
    'django.contrib.contenttypes',
    'django.contrib.sessions',
    'django.contrib.messages',
    'django.contrib.staticfiles',
    'home',      # app already included
]

MIDDLEWARE = [
    'django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware',
    'django.contrib.sessions.middleware.SessionMiddleware',
    'django.middleware.common.CommonMiddleware',
    'django.middleware.csrf.CsrfViewMiddleware',
    'django.contrib.auth.middleware.AuthenticationMiddleware',
    'django.contrib.messages.middleware.MessageMiddleware',
    'django.middleware.clickjacking.XFrameOptionsMiddleware',
]

ROOT_URLCONF = 'templateRendering.urls'

TEMPLATES = [
    {
        'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
        'DIRS': [],
        'APP_DIRS': True,
        'OPTIONS': {
            'context_processors': [
                'django.template.context_processors.debug',
                'django.template.context_processors.request',
                'django.contrib.auth.context_processors.auth',
                'django.contrib.messages.context_processors.messages',
            ],
        },
    },
]

WSGI_APPLICATION = 'templateRendering.wsgi.application'

DATABASES = {
    'default': {
        'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
        'NAME': BASE_DIR / 'db.sqlite3',
    }
}

STATIC_URL = '/static/'

Implement Pagination in a RESTful API
code:
from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
from data import items   # Importing the items list from data.py file

app = Flask(__name__)

# Route to fetch items using pagination
# Example request: /items?page=1&limit=3
# define the route function get_items() here

@app.route('/items',methods=['GET'])
def get_items():
	page = request.args.get('page', default=1, type=int)
	limit = request.args.get('limit', default=3, type=int)
	start = (page-1)*limit
	end = start+limit
	paginated_items = items[start:end]
	return jsonify({
		"page": page,
		"limit": limit,
		"results": paginated_items
	})

# Run the Flask application
if __name__ == "__main__":
	app.run(host="0.0.0.0", debug=True)
